ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No specific FoodEx2 code exists for garlic bread; A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of garlic bread is approximately one slice or piece weighing around 50 grams, as commonly served as a side dish or starter.

nutrient_source.title: Garlic bread, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 18963)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central entry for garlic bread (commercially prepared) used as the primary nutrient reference. Values adapted to represent a homemade garlic bread on Turkish ekmek (white bread base with garlic and butter/oil topping), which is nutritionally comparable. Sodium may be slightly lower in homemade versions. Values are per 100g edible portion.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — white bread GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for garlic ekmek. GI of 73 is assigned based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for white bread (GI 73, glucose reference scale), which is the primary structural component of this dish. Garlic and butter/oil toppings do not substantially alter the GI of the bread base. Glycemic load per 50g serving calculated as: (73 × 25.0g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 18.3.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit garlic bread intake as a single 50g serving contributes approximately 240 mg sodium (~10% of the 2300 mg daily limit), and restaurant versions may contain significantly more sodium from added salt and butter.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 73) and high refined carbohydrate content (~50g per 100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Garlic bread made from white bread has a high GI of 73 and a glycemic load of approximately 18 per serving, making it unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion control is essential and substitution with wholegrain bread is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium content from butter and salt
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Garlic bread prepared with butter contributes saturated fat (~3.5g per 100g) and sodium (~480 mg per 100g), both of which are risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals should prefer versions made with olive oil and limit portion size.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Garlic Ekmek, also known as garlic bread, is a popular bread-based side dish made from Turkish ekmek (a soft white leavened bread) topped with minced garlic and typically butter or olive oil, then baked or toasted until golden and fragrant. In the Middle Eastern and Turkish culinary tradition, ekmek refers to everyday bread, and the garlic-topped version is commonly served as a starter or accompaniment to grilled meats, kebabs, and shawarma. The texture is crispy on the outside with a soft, chewy interior, and the flavour is rich and savoury with a pronounced garlic aroma. Nutritionally, garlic bread is a moderate-to-high calorie food (~350 kcal per 100g) dominated by refined carbohydrates (~50g per 100g) and fat (~12g per 100g). The high glycemic index (GI 73) makes it a concern for individuals managing blood glucose levels. Sodium content (~480 mg per 100g) is relevant for hypertension management. Garlic itself contributes minor amounts of vitamin C, allicin (a bioactive compound with potential cardiovascular benefits), and trace minerals. In Singapore, this dish is served at Middle Eastern restaurants such as Sahara Bar and Restaurant and is typically ordered as a side dish or starter alongside main courses.
common_user_queries: How many calories in garlic bread?
Is garlic bread suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore or ICMR-NIN entry exists for garlic ekmek. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central garlic bread (commercially prepared), which is a defensible analogous match for a homemade white-bread-based garlic bread. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database white bread reference (GI 73), which is the appropriate analogous food. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted (not exact) source match.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for garlic bread. Vitamin B12 is 0.0 as garlic bread contains no animal-derived ingredients in its base formulation (bread, garlic, plant oil); this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. All other -1 values represent nutrients not reported in the primary source. GI of 73 is an analogous estimate based on white bread (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for garlic ekmek was found in approved sources. Sodium value (~480 mg/100g) reflects commercially prepared garlic bread; homemade versions at restaurants may vary. The food is served at Sahara Bar and Restaurant (58 Boat Quay, Singapore) as sourced from field survey data data.
